title: Workflow
description: Owl workflow source body. Stages 8 and post-MVP Stage 1 fix the contracts for the optional `publishes` field and for `steps[*]` item shape (including the `skill:` binding consumed by `step invocation`); per-step `context` / `context_file` (mutually exclusive) were added in the backend-abstraction refactor for the universal step runner. The rest of the workflow body is intentionally left open (additionalProperties: true) until a later stage tightens the full schema.

execution_mode.description: Workflow-level autonomy policy honored by discussion-step skills. `autonomous_after_brief` prompts the user only on the `brief` step; `autonomous` never prompts except on real blockers; `interactive` confirms each discussion step. Absent => treated as `interactive`.

allowed_children.description: Whitelist of child workflow keys; meaningful only on kind: composite_task. Absent => permissive (any child allowed). Empty array => strict deny (no children allowed). Non-empty array => enumerated whitelist.

id.description: Step identifier, unique within a workflow.

session_type.description: Logical session kind for this step. `discussion` runs in the main agent session and may interact with the user; `execution` runs in an isolated subagent session and never interacts directly. See RFC #1 (knowledge entry 46) §2.

publishes.description: Marks this step as the one that performs `owl publish` (the publish step gate resolves the publishing step by this flag, falling back to a step literally named `publish`). At most one step per workflow should set it.

verify.description: Opts this step into the objective verification gate: at `owl step complete` Owl runs `settings.verification.command` itself, authors the `verification` artifact from the exit code, and refuses completion unless the objective status is `passed` (fail-open with a warning when no command is configured). Mirrors the `publishes` step-marker precedent; the command is never stored in the workflow.

tier.description: Optional model tier hint. Defaults: discussion→advanced, execution→standard. Mapping tier→model lives in env-specific config (~/.config/owl/tier_map.yaml or $OWL_TIER_MAP_PATH). See RFC #1 §3.

drift_policy.description: Per-step content-SHA drift handling (RFC #1 §4). `block` returns exit 2 + drift_block payload and aborts the step transition; `warn` writes a stderr message and continues; `ignore` is silent. When the step does not declare a policy, the default is `block` for execution-typed steps and `warn` for discussion-typed steps.

skill.description: Skill id consumed verbatim by `owl step invocation` so agents can locate the matching SKILL.md on disk. Seeded session-typed workflows bind `owl-step-discussion` or `owl-step-execution`; custom workflows may name their own `owl-step-<x>` skill as long as it matches the pattern.

requires.description: Ids of steps that must be completed (or skipped) before this step becomes ready.

creates.description: Artifact type keys this step is expected to produce; validated by `owl artifact validate` at step completion.

optional.description: When true, the step may be skipped via `owl step skip --reason ...` without blocking downstream steps.

context.description: Inline per-step context shown to the universal step runner. Mutually exclusive with `context_file`.

context_file.description: Relative path (from the workflow source directory) to a Markdown file containing per-step context. Resolved through Workflows::Backend. Mutually exclusive with `context` and `variants`.

variants.description: Declared alternative implementations of this step. When present, the running task chooses one (via `--variant <name>` or `default_variant`) and the resolver loads that variant's `context_file`. Mutually exclusive with the step's own `context` / `context_file`.

default_variant.description: Variant key used when the running task does not select one explicitly. Required when `variants` is present, must be one of its keys.

context_file.description: Relative path to the Markdown context file for this variant, resolved like the step-level `context_file`.

title.description: Optional human-readable label for the variant (used by orchestrator UIs to present a chooser).

from.description: Source path template, relative to the `tasks` storage role. Supports {{task.id}} and {{task.slug}}.

to.description: Destination path template, relative to the `docs` storage role. Supports {{task.id}} and {{task.slug}}.

optional.description: When true, a missing source artifact is a clean no-op (action: skipped_missing_source) instead of a source_missing error. Use for rules whose artifact is optional (e.g. the feature workflow's design.md).
